title: Cochlidium paucinervatum
intro: Cochlidium paucinervatum is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America, characterized by its delicate fronds and slender stems. Traditionally, Cochlidium paucinervatum is used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Cochlidium paucinervatum is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tinctures and salves.
taxonomy: Cochlidium paucinervatum is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cochlidium, which is a group of tropical ferns. This species is native to Central and South America, and it is characterized by its small size and delicate fronds. Cochlidium paucinervatum is often found growing on tree trunks and branches in humid, subtropical environments. The classification of Cochlidium paucinervatum reflects its unique characteristics and its position within the larger family of Polypodiaceae.
